cinematicHero.headline: KERWIN
cinematicHero.taglineLine1: TypeScript full stack—maintainable systems that ship.
cinematicHero.taglineLine2: What runs and iterates matters more than buzzwords.

philosophy.body: From rough prototypes to production: TypeScript-first stacks, clear boundaries between UI and services, and documentation that survives real handoffs—not just the first demo.
philosophy.leftImageAlt: Product still in warm outdoor light

featured.titleLine2: Designed for the next ten releases.

items.title: End-to-end delivery from idea to deploy
LLM pipelines, RAG & agents where they belong
Blog notes on tradeoffs, pitfalls and tooling

technology.revealText: I care about systems you can refactor without fear: explicit contracts, tests where they buy confidence, and observability when things go wrong in production. Most of my work sits between product UX and reliable backends—shipping features without painting the team into a corner.

items.title: Types as documentation
items.description: Lean on TypeScript at boundaries so refactors stay boring.

items.title: Tests that earn their keep
items.description: Targeted coverage around money paths and tricky state—not vanity metrics.

items.title: Docs someone will read
items.description: READMEs, ADRs and runbooks written for the next maintainer.

items.title: Composable UI
items.description: Design systems and components with sane constraints and a11y in mind.

items.title: Automation over ceremony
items.description: Scripts, templates and CI steps to remove repeated human error.

items.title: Performance you can prove
items.description: Profile, measure, fix hotspots—then stop when good enough.

statement.quote: I'm Kerwin—a TS full-stack engineer from China. I like shipping maintainable systems with React and Node, squeezing real value out of LLMs where it fits, and turning late-night debugging into stories on the blog.

footer.brandTagline: TypeScript, React, Node and pragmatic AI—shipping maintainable systems and writing about the messy middle.

hero.description: “A full-stack engineer who’s evolved from 2019’s ‘cut-and-paste’ to 2026’s ‘AI training master,’ hopelessly stuck in TypeScript’s ‘type hell,’ building systems that even AI would call ‘elegant,’ and writing about every late-night debugging session as a joke on the blog.”

about.heading: Who's behind all this
about.intro: I'm Kerwin, from China. Evolved from web dev to front-end to full-stack to TS Full-Stack. I care more about shipping and iterating than stacking buzzwords.
about.skillDesc: Front-end: responsive layouts done right. Back-end: rock-solid architecture. AI: model fine-tuning, RAG in production, and teaching agents when to shut up.
about.outputDesc: I write about real-world pitfalls, architecture tradeoffs, and tooling so I can reflect and hopefully help others.

services.heading: My
services.subtitle: Front-end, back-end, full-stack delivery, and DevOps—plus ongoing writing and sharing.
services.frontendDesc: React / Next.js / TypeScript, components, state, performance, and accessibility.
services.backendDesc: Node.js services, REST/GraphQL, database modeling, and optimization.
services.fullstackDesc: End-to-end from requirements to launch: architecture, APIs, integration, and release.
services.devopsDesc: Docker, CI/CD, monitoring, and logging for stable, traceable iterations.
services.writingDesc: Blog posts on pitfalls, tradeoffs, and tooling for reflection and sharing.
services.contactDesc: Have a project or want to chat? Drop an email or leave a message on the blog—I’ll likely be able to help.

portfolio.heading: My
portfolio.project1Desc: React + Node full-stack: permissions, workflows, charts, CI/CD, and monitoring.
portfolio.project2Desc: Next.js SSG/ISR, Markdown blog, simple CMS, SEO and maintainability.
portfolio.project3Desc: Salesforce-based middleware: unified APIs, data sync, and workflow orchestration for multi-system integration and ops.
portfolio.project4Desc: Intelligent search powered by sample-based learning and behavior analysis: semantic search, intent recognition, and automated discovery for data and knowledge exploration.
portfolio.project5Desc: Lightweight, highly customizable AI Agent editor (Electron + React). My contributions: UI layout & Mac window controls, terminal crash fix & error handling, Markdown table rendering with remark-gfm (#14), type safety & Logger refactor, updater hang fix.
portfolio.project6Desc: Open multi-agent market intelligence platform: Researcher, Analyst, and Writer agents work together for competitive research. FastAPI + React + TypeScript, real-time streaming, Bright Data scraping. Live demo on Vercel.

experience.heading: Career
experience.subtitle: From 0 to 1 to ∞. Northeastern University (China) grad—built to ship in the cold and in the codebase.
experience.exp1Desc: Code? That’s AI’s job now. Mine is teaching AI to behave: LLM-powered pipelines, “code that writes code.”
experience.exp1Motto: If AI replaces my job, it's because I didn't train it well enough.
experience.exp2Desc: Realized that fixing the front alone wasn't enough—had to fix the pipes too. One moment tuning CSS animations, the next writing SQL injection defenses.
experience.exp2Motto: Hair density down, sense of control at an all-time high.
experience.exp3RoleTags: Browser performance squeezer / Vue & React dual cultivator
experience.exp3Desc: Northeastern University grad, dove into the front-end deep end. Survived the framework wars and node_modules black holes; stopped obsessing over pixels, started obsessing over components.
experience.exp3Motto: First instinct on any webpage: F12.
experience.exp4Title: Web newbie era · "if it runs, ship it" engineer
experience.exp4RoleTags: HTML skeleton / CSS makeup / JS magic (spells in progress)
experience.exp4Desc: Simple pages and maintenance—forms, styles, compatibility. Watched console.log go from debug tool to religion.
experience.exp4Motto: Why did that div fall down again?

testimonials.heading: What my clients say
testimonials.subtitle: Feedback and reviews from partners and readers.
testimonials.quote: Worked with Kerwin on several projects—clear delivery, pragmatic tech choices, and docs that actually get updated. Low-friction collaboration.

footer.siteDescription: From web to front-end to full-stack to TS Full-Stack. I build maintainable systems with TypeScript, React, Node, and LLMs, and write about it on my blog.
